title: Glandularia hassleriana
intro: Glandularia hassleriana, also known as Yerba Buena, is a flowering plant native to the temperate regions of South America, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, making it a potential treatment for various conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Glandularia hassleriana include volatile oils, flavonoids, and terpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are the most common preparations used to harness its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Glandularia hassleriana is classified within the family Verbenaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the vervain or verbena family. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and its genetic relationships to other members of the family. The genus Glandularia is a group of flowering plants that are native to South America. Glandularia hassleriana is a specific species within this genus and is native to Paraguay. Glandularia hassleriana is also known as Paraguayan verbena or verbena.
